name: Company or business entity incorporation (DCIP)
notes: Supported by the Black Book licence record's applicability to formal business organisations. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Formal business entities register with the Department of Companies and Intellectual Property, and private companies file annual returns.
appliesWhen: On formation of a formal business entity, and annually thereafter for the annual return.

name: ZIMRA taxpayer registration
notes: Supported by the Black Book record's applicability to individuals, companies, partnerships and cooperatives starting a business. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: New businesses register with ZIMRA through TaRMS within the period stated in ZIMRA's new-trader guidance.

name: VAT registration and returns (ZIMRA)
notes: Applicability and return cadences per the Black Book records; the current registration threshold is deliberately not restated here — confirm with ZIMRA. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Persons trading in taxable supplies who meet the compulsory registration rules register for VAT and file returns for their assigned tax period.
appliesWhen: When the business meets the current compulsory VAT registration rules; returns per assigned tax period thereafter.

name: Employer (PAYE) registration and remittance (ZIMRA)
notes: Supported by the Black Book record's applicability to businesses that become employers. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Businesses that become employers register for PAYE, remit monthly, and file the annual ITF 16 return.
appliesWhen: On becoming an employer; monthly remittance and annual return thereafter.

name: NSSA employer registration and POBS contributions
notes: Supported by the Black Book record's applicability to employers of labour. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Employers of labour register with NSSA and remit Pensions and Other Benefits Scheme contributions monthly.
appliesWhen: On becoming an employer of labour; monthly remittance thereafter.

name: PRAZ supplier/bidder registration for public works
notes: Directly supported by the Black Book record's applicability to suppliers, bidders and contractors seeking public-procurement eligibility. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Contractors bidding for public construction works register with PRAZ as suppliers/bidders and renew each calendar year.
appliesWhen: Before bidding for public procurement; registration runs to 31 December and renews annually.

name: ESIA certificate for listed projects (EMA)
notes: Whether a specific project is in an EMA-listed category must be confirmed against EMA's current prescribed list. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Construction projects in EMA-listed categories with potential negative environmental effects require an ESIA certificate before implementation, with quarterly progress reports and renewal rules while conditions apply.
appliesWhen: Before implementing a project in an EMA-listed category; quarterly reporting and renewal rules per certificate conditions.

name: Business membership bodies relevant to construction
notes: Membership is voluntary; sector-specific construction federations are recorded as gaps below. Source URLs inherited from the Zimbabwe Black Book dataset (record lastReviewed 2026-07-15); not independently re-fetched in this revision.
obligation: Contractors may voluntarily join cross-sector bodies such as ZNCC and the employers' confederation EMCOZ.

name: Municipal building plan approval and inspections
notes: Declared research gap: council plan-approval processes exist under building by-laws but are not yet evidenced by a Black Book record; the referenced councils are examples of the authority class. No fee, form, or deadline is asserted.
description: Local authorities approve building plans and inspect works under building by-laws and planning law; the Black Book has no licence-type record yet for plan approval, inspection stages or occupation certificates.

name: Public-works contractor registration/categorisation
notes: Declared research gap: believed relevant from general industry knowledge but not yet evidenced by a Black Book record. No fee, form, or deadline is asserted. Candidate for a future PB content mission.
description: Registration or categorisation of building and civil contractors for public works (historically administered under the Ministry responsible for public works) is not yet evidenced by a Black Book record.

name: Construction industry federation membership
notes: Declared research gap: believed relevant from general industry knowledge but not yet evidenced by a Black Book record. No fee, form, or deadline is asserted. Candidate for a future PB content mission.
description: Sector membership bodies for contractors (for example a construction industry federation) are not yet represented in the Black Book's business associations.

name: National Employment Council for the construction industry
notes: Declared research gap: believed relevant from general industry knowledge but not yet evidenced by a Black Book record. No fee, form, or deadline is asserted. Candidate for a future PB content mission.
description: Sector collective-bargaining obligations (minimum wages, levies and union dues administered through the industry's employment council) are not yet evidenced by a Black Book record.

name: Contract withholding tax and tax clearance (ITF 263)
notes: Declared research gap: believed relevant from general industry knowledge but not yet evidenced by a Black Book record. No fee, form, or deadline is asserted. Candidate for a future PB content mission.
description: Withholding on contract payments to suppliers without a valid tax clearance, and the tax clearance certificate itself, are not yet documented as Black Book records.
